questions.title: Reading Text 1: Koalas – Fascinating Australian Animals
questions.content: Koalas are unique animals found only in Australia. They are marsupials, which means they carry their babies in a pouch. Koalas spend most of their time in eucalyptus trees, eating leaves. These leaves are tough and not very nutritious, so koalas sleep up to 20 hours a day to save energy. A baby koala, called a joey, stays in its mother's pouch for about six months. After that, it rides on her back until it's ready to be on its own. Koalas have sharp claws for climbing and soft fur that is grey or brown. Sadly, koalas are vulnerable because of habitat loss from bushfires and land clearing. People are working to protect them by planting more trees and creating safe areas.

questions.title: Reading Text 2: The Lost Treasure Hunt
questions.content: Mia and her brother Alex were exploring their grandparents' attic on a rainy day. 'Look at this old map!' Mia exclaimed, holding up a dusty paper with strange symbols. 'It looks like a treasure map,' Alex said excitedly. The map showed a path through the nearby forest to a hidden cave. They decided to follow it, grabbing torches and snacks. In the forest, they crossed a stream and climbed a hill. Suddenly, they heard rustling in the bushes. It was just a rabbit! Finally, they reached the cave. Inside, they found an old chest filled with shiny coins and jewels. But the real treasure was a note from their great-grandfather: 'The best adventures are shared with family.' Mia and Alex smiled, knowing they had found something special.

questions.title: Reading Text 3: Why We Should Recycle
questions.content: Recycling is important for our planet. Every day, we throw away things like plastic bottles, paper, and cans. If we don't recycle, these items go to landfills where they take hundreds of years to break down. This harms the environment and animals. By recycling, we save resources like trees for paper and oil for plastic. It also reduces pollution from making new products. For example, recycling one tonne of paper saves 17 trees! At home, you can sort your rubbish into bins for recycling. Schools can have recycling programs too. If everyone recycles, we can make the Earth cleaner and healthier for future generations. Start recycling today – it's easy and makes a big difference!
